DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: JONATHAN A. GLASS FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY: AMERICAN HERITAGE LIFE INSURANCE CO. SAM WARE FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: MARC BERMUDEZ BY CONSENT OF PARTIES TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT FINDS GOOD CAUSE TO ORDER THAT THE RE-NOTICED DEPOSITION OF THE PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE BE CONDUCTED EITHER BY TELEPHONE OR VIDEO-CONFERENCE. IF DEFENDANT HAS ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS THAT WERE NOT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED PURSUANT TO THE ORIGINAL NOTICE OF DEPOSITION, SAID DOCUMENTS SHALL BE PROVIDED TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF AT LEAST 2 DAYS BEFORE THE DEPOSITION. THE ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS, IF ANY, SHALL BE IN A FORMAT OR MEDIUM THAT FACILITATES THEIR USE DURING THE DEPOSITION. THE DEPOSITION SHALL BE SCHEDULED AT A TIME MUTUALLY CONVENIENT TO THE PARTIES AND IS TO BE COMPLETED BY FEBRUARY 25, 2005. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
